published.text:
### Hero
Heading: AI Phone Answering for Service Businesses
Body: Your phone rings and nobody picks up. The caller hangs up and calls your competitor. This happens dozens of times per week for busy service businesses — during jobs, after hours, on weekends. AI phone answering means every call gets answered, every time. Not a voicemail. Not an answering service. An AI agent that can answer questions, qualify the caller, and book appointments directly into your calendar.
Sub: Below: what AI phone answering actually does, what callers experience, where it works well, and where it does not.

### Core sections
- What AI phone answering handles (6 capabilities)
- AI phone answering vs alternatives (comparison table)
- What callers actually experience (considerations section)

### FAQ
Subhead: Practical answers about AI phone answering for service businesses

### Final CTA
Heading: Stop losing callers to voicemail
Body: Book a 30-minute call. We will look at your call volume, where calls are being missed, and whether AI phone answering would pay off for your business.

edits.description: Published as part of batch 3. Broadens the voice-agent cluster beyond restaurants.
Includes comparison table (AI vs answering service vs voicemail) for decision-making.
Interlinks to AI voice agent for restaurants, missed call follow-up, and AI appointment setter.
Addresses caller experience directly — voice quality, interruptions, handoffs.
